paper_metadata.title: Pythia: A Suite for Analyzing Large Language Models Across Training and Scaling
paper_metadata.abstract: How do large language models (LLMs) develop and evolve over the course of training? How do these patterns change as models scale? To answer these questions, we introduce \textit{Pythia}, a suite of 16 LLMs all trained on public data seen in the exact same order and ranging in size from 70M to 12B parameters. We provide public access to 154 checkpoints for each one of the 16 models, alongside tools to download and reconstruct their exact training dataloaders for further study. We intend \textit{Pythia} to facilitate research in many areas, and we present several case studies including novel results in memorization, term frequency effects on few-shot performance, and reducing gender bias. We demonstrate that this highly controlled setup can be used to yield novel insights toward LLMs and their training dynamics. Trained models, analysis code, training code, and training data can be found at \url{https://github.com/EleutherAI/pythia}.

extraction_data.innovation: Pythia is a suite of models designed to enable reproducible and consistent research on large language models. It uses a Transformer architecture with consistent training procedures across scales, including deduplicated data, parallelized attention, and untied embeddings.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Pile (deduplicated version of English language datasets)
extraction_data.extension: The paper introduces a method to modify training data by swapping gendered pronouns to test the impact on bias, using a controlled retraining setup to isolate the effect of pronoun frequency.
extraction_data.research_problem: Understanding the effects of data bias and scaling in large language models
extraction_data.application: Large language model analysis, interpretability, and bias mitigation
extraction_data.paper_title: Pythia: A Suite for Analyzing Large Language Models Across Training and Scaling

extraction_data.innovation: Pythia models are trained on a large-scale text corpus and then partially retrained with gender-swapped pronouns to study the impact of corpus statistics on bias. This approach uses a controlled setup to isolate the effect of pronoun frequency on downstream bias.
extraction_data.application: Bias analysis and mitigation in large language models
extraction_data.research_problem: Understanding how corpus statistics influence bias in large language models
extraction_data.extension: The paper introduces a method to modify training data by swapping gendered pronouns to test the impact on bias, using a controlled retraining setup to isolate the effect of pronoun frequency.
extraction_data.paper_title: Pythia: A Suite for Analyzing Large Language Models Across Training and Scaling
